title: Misery
review: I have never read a Steven King book but to me they all seem like cool horror ideas, executed without quite as many twists or character development as I would like. It's as though he's the king of the premise, but not of the ending. This film is centred around a great idea, but nothing in the second or third act really surprised me. James Caan is both confident and capable, with his usual level of machismo, as well as helpless His captor was intimidating, as well as vulnerable. They're good characters but it plays out just you'd expect it to.
